title: By Senator Rosilicie Ochoa Bogh, 23rd Senate District
prompt_preview:

Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Breast cancer is the most commonly diagnosed cancer among American women.",
    "About one in eight women will be diagnosed with invasive breast cancer in her lifetime.",
    "In the U.S. in 2021, 281,550 new cases of invasive breast cancer and 49,290 cases of non-invasive breast cancer will be diagnosed in women.",
    "About 43,600 women are projected to die from breast cancer in the U.S. in 2021.",
    "Breast cancer is the second leading cause of cancer-related deaths among women, after lung cancer.",
    "About 2,650 new cases of invasive breast cancer will be diagnosed in men in 2021.",
    "A man’s lifetime risk of breast cancer is about 1 in 833.",
    "White and Black women get breast cancer at rates of 132 and 125 per 100,000, respectively.",
    "The death rate from breast cancer among Black women is almost 40% higher than that of White women.",
    "The incidence of breast cancer is lower among Asian/Pacific Islanders (105 per 100,000) and Latinas (100 per 100,000), and even lower among Native Americans/Native Alaskans (82 per 100,000).",
    "Native Americans/Native Alaskans have a higher breast cancer death rate (15 per 100,000) compared to Latinas (14 per 100,000) or Asians/Pacific Islanders (12 per 100,000).",
    "Early detection of breast cancer increases the chances of successful treatment.",
    "Mammography is the single most effective method of detecting breast changes that may be cancer.",
    "Mammography can find breast cancer up to 10 years before it would be large enough to feel during a self-examination.",
    "Increased awareness and screening have led to increasing rates of early detection of breast cancer.",
    "Breast cancer death rates have decreased due to early detections and better, more targeted treatments.",
    "Between the years of 2013-2018, the decrease in breast cancer death rates has been a small but steady 1% per year overall.",
    "The main risk factors for breast cancer include being a woman, being over age 50, and experiencing changes in BRCA1 or BRCA2 genes."
  ]
}
```
